functional_requirements.title: Add Book
functional_requirements.description: User can add a new book with title and author. Each book gets a unique ID and default status 'available'.

functional_requirements.title: List Books
functional_requirements.description: User can list all books, optionally filtered by status (available/borrowed/all). Output includes ID, title, author, status.

functional_requirements.title: Borrow Book
functional_requirements.description: User can borrow a book by ID. Checks that book exists and is available, then changes status to 'borrowed'.

functional_requirements.title: Return Book
functional_requirements.description: User can return a book by ID. Checks that book exists and is borrowed, then changes status to 'available'.

functional_requirements.title: Remove Book
functional_requirements.description: User can delete a book by ID. Checks that book exists and is not currently borrowed.

non_functional_requirements.description: All five operations must execute correctly with proper state transitions.
Error handling must be comprehensive: book not found, duplicate borrow, etc.

acceptance_criteria.description: Add book: book is added with unique ID and status 'available'.
List books: returns correct list with optional status filter.
Borrow book: changes status from 'available' to 'borrowed'.
Return book: changes status from 'borrowed' to 'available'.
Remove book: deletes book only if not borrowed.
Error handling: appropriate error messages for invalid operations.

storage.details: No persistence; data lost on session end.

glossary.book: A record containing title, author, unique ID, and borrow status.
glossary.available: Status indicating the book is not borrowed.
glossary.borrowed: Status indicating the book is currently borrowed.

global_state_sources.description: Stores all books with book_id as key.
Counter for generating unique book IDs.

input_spec.description: Input is a JSON object with command and book_data fields.

output_spec.description: Output is a JSON object with success, message, and data fields.
